topic.name: Offshore Oil Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and liability of offshore oil drilling and pollution in U.S. waters.

Document 1: requires all vessels engaged in drilling for oil or gas in ocean waters to be documented
protection of coastal waters and shorelines
Document 2: amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990
makes the party responsible for an offshore facility liable for all discharge removal costs
Document 3: amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990
liability for all discharge removal costs and damages for each incident

topic.name: Youth Employment and Mentoring Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing summer employment opportunities and mentorship for youth through federal grants and public-private partnerships.

Document 1: direct the Department of Labor to award grants to states to help local areas with high concentrations of eligible youth
provide those youth with summer employment opportunities directly linked to academic and occupational learning.
Document 2: direct the Corporation for National and Community Service to award competitive, matching, three-year grants to nonpartisan, collaborative, public-private partnerships
for efforts to grow and strengthen direct-service youth mentoring programs in all forms and settings in their service areas.
Document 3: direct the Corporation for National and Community Service to award competitive, matching, three-year grants to nonpartisan, collaborative, public-private partnerships
for efforts to grow and strengthen direct-service youth mentoring programs in all forms and settings in their service areas.

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating oil and gas leasing and production activities on the Outer Continental Shelf, particularly in relation to royalty incentives and environmental protections.

Document 1: prohibit oil and gas preleasing, leasing, and related activities in areas of the Outer Continental Shelf located off the coast of California
exclude such areas from the waters of the U.S. Outer Continental Shelf
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil or gas lease sale under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act with royalty-based incentives
repeal royalty relief for deep water oil and gas production in the Western and Central Planning Area of the Gulf of Mexico
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing any oil or gas lease sale under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act with royalty-based incentives
repeal royalty relief for deep water oil and gas production in the Western and Central Planning Area of the Gulf of Mexico

topic.name: Ninth Circuit Court Reorganization
topic.description: Legislation concerning the division and reorganization of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit.

Document 1: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into: (1) the Ninth Circuit, composed of California, Guam, Hawaii, and Northern Mariana Islands; and (2) the Twelfth Circuit, composed of Alaska, Arizona, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington.
Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit, three judges for the new Ninth Circuit, and two additional temporary judges for the former Ninth Circuit.
Document 2: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into: (1) the Ninth Circuit, composed of California, Guam, Hawaii, and Northern Mariana Islands; and (2) the Twelfth Circuit, composed of Alaska, Arizona, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington.
Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit, three additional judges for the new Ninth Circuit, and two temporary judges for the former Ninth Circuit.
Document 3: Divides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit into: (1) the Ninth Circuit, composed of California, Guam, Hawaii, and Northern Mariana Islands; and (2) the Twelfth Circuit, composed of Alaska, Arizona, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, and Washington.
Directs the President to appoint two additional judges for the former Ninth Circuit, three judges for the new Ninth Circuit, and two additional temporary judges for the former Ninth Circuit.

topic.name: Federal Jurisdiction and Legal Procedures
topic.description: This topic encompasses the jurisdictional rules and legal procedures related to federal courts, particularly regarding claims against the United States and national security investigations.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in, or on appeal from, the U.S. Court of Federal Claims.
Requires the court presiding over the action that was filed later to stay the later action until the action that was filed first is no longer pending.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in, or on appeal from, the U.S. Court of Federal Claims.
Provides the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit with jurisdiction of an appeal from an interlocutory order terminating or modifying such a stay.
Document 3: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend provisions granting roving electronic surveillance authority.
Requires courts, in considering whether to grant a nondisclosure order, to give substantial weight to the facts alleged by the government in its certification.

topic.name: Medicaid Access for Practitioners
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the role of advanced practice nurses and physician assistants within the Medicaid system.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Includes nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists, physician assistants, certified nurse midwives, and certified registered nurse anesthetists
Document 2: Eliminate the state option to include nurse practitioners, certified nurse-midwives, and physician assistants as primary care case managers
Revises the coverage of certain nurse practitioner services under the Medicaid fee-for-service program
Document 3: Specifies as primary care case managers any nurse practitioner, certified nurse-midwife, or physician assistant
Extends coverage to services furnished by a nurse practitioner or clinical nurse specialist

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and extensions related to unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation
Document 2: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation through August 27, 2009
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act
extends emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before April 1, 2014

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and consolidation of private and federal education loans, including borrower protections and obligations.

Document 1: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
requires institutions of higher education to provide borrowers of federal education loans information at their entrance counseling
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program
Document 3: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require lenders of private education loans to describe clearly and conspicuously, in writing, the cosigners' obligations regarding such loans
requires institutions of higher education to provide borrowers of federal education loans information at their entrance counseling

topic.name: Federal Aviation Administration Funding
topic.description: Legislation extending funding and provisions for aviation-related projects and taxes in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through March 31, 2010: (1) excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation of persons and property;
(2) the expenditure authority for the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through FY2009: (1) excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation of persons and property;
(2) the expenditure authority for the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through April 30, 2010: (1) excise taxes on aviation fuels and air transportation of persons and property;
(2) the expenditure authority for the Airport and Airway Trust Fund.

topic.name: Child Recovery and DNA Analysis
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the recovery of missing children and enhancing DNA analysis for law enforcement purposes.

Document 1: A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center Act
assist federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies in recovering missing children
Document 2: A Child Is Missing Alert and Recovery Center Act
assist federal, state, and local law enforcement agencies in recovering missing children
Document 3: Debbie Smith Reauthorization Act of 2008
authorize appropriations for DNA analysis grant programs

topic.name: Alternative Fuel Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at promoting the use of alternative and renewable fuels in transportation to reduce reliance on traditional fossil fuels.

Document 1: Open Fuel Standard Act of 2011 requires manufacturers to produce a certain percentage of qualified vehicles.
Defines 'qualified vehicle' to include those operating on natural gas, hydrogen, biodiesel, and electric drive.
Document 2: Renewable Fuel Standard Elimination Act amends the Clean Air Act to repeal the renewable fuel program.
Document 3: Domestic Alternative Fuels Act of 2012 revises the Renewable Fuel Program to include domestic alternative fuels.
Defines 'domestic alternative fuel' as ethanol produced from domestic fossil fuels other than petroleum.

topic.name: Labor Rights and Protections
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation aimed at enhancing labor rights, workplace safety, and employee protections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) by a majority of the employees in a unit.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.

Requires the NLRB to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees of an appropriate unit, whether such unit shall be the employer unit, craft unit, plant unit, or subdivision unit.

topic.name: Veterans Chiropractic Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing chiropractic care services to veterans through Department of Veterans Affairs medical centers.

Document 1: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act of 2011
requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans
Document 2: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act
requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans
Document 3: Chiropractic Care Available to All Veterans Act of 2015
requires a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans

topic.name: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission's role in managing energy projects and regional transmission planning.

Document 1: Authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to: (1) reinstate the license for either or each of the projects numbered 10822 and 10823;
Directs FERC to: (1) transfer the reinstated licenses to the town of Canton, Connecticut.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to designate one or more regions within the Eastern Interconnection and the Western Interconnection to be represented by specified regional transmission planners.
Directs FERC to require that: (1) all regional high voltage electric transmission cost allocation processes and methodologies adhere to a clear and consistent set of specified regulatory principles;

topic.name: Second Amendment Rights
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reinforcing and clarifying the rights of individuals to possess and use firearms in the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 2: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 3: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it or any other provision of law shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council, the Mayor, or any governmental or regulatory authority of the District to prohibit, constructively prohibit, or unduly burden the ability of persons otherwise not prohibited from possessing firearms under federal law from acquiring, possessing in their homes or businesses, transporting for legitimate purposes, or using for sporting, self-protection or other lawful purposes, any firearm neither prohibited by federal law nor subject to the National Firearms Act.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws or regulations that discourage or eliminate the private ownership or use of firearms.

topic.name: Data Broker Accountability and Security
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating data brokers to ensure the protection and accuracy of personal information while establishing protocols for breach notifications.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses data containing personal information to establish specified security policies and procedures.
Makes it unlawful for information brokers to obtain or disclose personal information by false pretenses.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each covered entity that owns or possesses data containing personal information to implement policies and procedures regarding information security practices.
Establishes procedures in the event of an information security breach.
Document 3: Data brokers must establish procedures to ensure the accuracy of the personal information they collect, assemble, or maintain.
Individuals may dispute the accuracy of their personal information with a written request that the data broker make a correction.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Small Businesses
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives specifically designed to support small businesses and S corporations.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax treatment of S corporations
allowing S corporations to increase passive investment income from 25 to 60% without incurring additional tax
Document 2: make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
allow a qualified small business to use a portion of its tax credit for increasing research expenditures as an offset against its employment tax liability
Document 3: increase the exclusion from gross income of the gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock from 50 to 100%
repeal gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock as an item of tax preference for purposes of the alternative minimum tax

topic.name: Invasive Species Management
topic.description: Legislative measures and programs aimed at controlling and eradicating invasive species to protect ecosystems and restore affected environments.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp
support to state and local government strategies, to slow, and eventually eliminate, the threat posed by such carp.
Document 2: Establishes a nutria eradication program under which the Secretary of the Interior may provide financial assistance
to eradicate or control nutria and restore nutria-damaged wetland.
Document 3: Amends the Nutria Eradication and Control Act of 2003 to revise the nutria eradication program
Establishes the goals of such Program as: (1) eradicating nutria in Maryland; (2) eradicating or controlling nutria in Louisiana, Delaware, North Carolina, Oregon, Virginia, and Washington.

topic.name: Currency Valuation and Trade Policy
topic.description: Legislation addressing the implications of undervalued foreign currencies on U.S. trade and countervailing duties.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Requires the administering authority to determine that the currency of a foreign country is fundamentally undervalued if for an 18-month period...
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Declares that the fact that such a subsidy is also provided in circumstances not involving export shall not...
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding the benefit conferred on merchandise imported into the United States from foreign countries with fundamentally undervalued currency.
Defines 'benefit conferred,' in cases where the currency of a foreign country is exchanged for foreign currency...

topic.name: Engineering and Computing Advancements
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at promoting research and development in engineering biology and high-performance computing technologies.

Document 1: Directs the President to implement a National Engineering Biology Research and Development Program
supporting social science research that advances the field of engineering biology
Document 2: Defines 'exascale computing' as computing through the use of a computing machine that performs near or above 10 to the 18th power floating point operations per second
conduct a research program to develop exascale computing machines
Document 3: Renames the Department of Energy High-End Computing Revitalization Act of 2004
establish national laboratory-industry partnerships for the research and development of exascale computing machines

topic.name: Global Women's Health and Empowerment
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative efforts and policies aimed at improving the health, safety, and empowerment of women and girls globally.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to establish an Office of Global Women's Issues
Authorizes the Secretary and the Administrator of USAID to provide assistance to prevent and respond to violence against women and girls internationally.
Document 3: Authorizes the President to provide assistance, including through international organizations, national governments, and international and local nongovernmental organizations, to address the social and health issues that lead to obstetric fistula.
Provides that such assistance shall promote the coordination facilitated by the International Obstetric Fistula Working Group.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts related to agricultural risk management and commodity production regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Aggregate Risk and Revenue Management Act of 2011 or the ARRM Act of 2011
repeals the direct and counter-cyclical payment programs for covered commodities
Document 2: Farming Flexibility Act of 2013
amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Virginia Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the federal recognition of various Indian tribes in Virginia and the implications of this recognition regarding their rights and eligibility for federal services.

Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe
Makes the Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.

topic.name: North Korea Sanctions and Diplomatic Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss the ongoing U.S. sanctions and diplomatic nonrecognition of North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism until specific conditions are met.

Document 1: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Continues diplomatic nonrecognition of North Korea until such benchmarks have been met.
Document 2: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Secretary of State should redesignate North Korea as a state sponsor of terrorism.
Document 3: Continues diplomatic, economic, and military sanctions against the government of North Korea as a supporter of international terrorism

Directs the President, in the case of a North Korean missile, rocket, or other airborne launch or the conducting of a nuclear test in violation of U.N. Security Council Resolutions, to instruct the U.S. Permanent Representative to the United Nations to use U.S. influence to secure adoption of a Security Council resolution condemning North Korea's action.

topic.name: Regulatory Impact Analysis
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative acts focused on the requirements and accountability of regulatory impact analyses for federal rules and independent regulatory agencies.

Document 1: require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
require federal agencies to prepare and publish in the Federal Register an initial and final regulatory impact analysis
Document 2: require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
require such agencies to identify and consider regulatory alternatives
Document 3: authorizes the President to require an independent regulatory agency to comply with regulatory analysis requirements
the President may require in independent regulatory agency to publish and provide the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs (OIRA) with an assessment of the costs and benefits of such rule

topic.name: Tax Policy and Reporting
topic.description: Legislation related to amendments in the Internal Revenue Code focusing on taxpayer responsibilities and reporting requirements.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the taxpayer election to deduct state and local general sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require reporting to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) of payments of $600 or more made to corporations.
set forth criteria and rules relating to the treatment of workers as employees or independent contractors.

topic.name: Federal Budget Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring transparency and accountability in federal budget allocations, particularly focusing on specific funds and programs.

Document 1: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Limits the use of such resources to such programs only.
Document 2: Requires the President's annual budget to Congress to include a detailed, separate analysis for the prior fiscal year, the current fiscal year, the fiscal year for which the budget is submitted, and the ensuing fiscal year identifying the amounts of gross and net appropriations or obligational authority and outlays directed to children and children's programs.
Document 3: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund for harbor maintenance programs to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund for that fiscal year.
Limits the use of such resources to such programs only.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative changes and amendments related to health care coverage and insurance provisions in the United States.

Document 1: Employee Health Care Protection Act of 2015 permits continued health coverage outside of health care exchanges.
Treats that coverage as a grandfathered health plan for maintaining minimum essential health coverage.
Document 2: Family Health Care Flexibility Act repeals restrictions on health savings accounts and flexible spending arrangements.
Allows payments for over-the-counter medications and removes contribution limits.
Document 3: Care for All Act of 2015 amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act.
Allows catastrophic plans to be offered as qualified health plans to a broader audience.

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health outcomes through targeted programs and funding for specific health risks and populations.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants to public and nonprofit private health care providers to provide treatment for diabetes in minority communities.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that such grants cover a variety of diabetes-related health care services.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), to establish and implement a birth defects prevention and public awareness program.
Includes a nationwide media campaign to increase awareness among health care providers and at-risk populations.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), to establish and implement a birth defects prevention and public awareness program.
Includes grants for the provision of, or campaigns to increase awareness about, pregnancy and breastfeeding information services.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: The documents focus on the establishment of mechanisms for evaluating and enhancing the accountability and effectiveness of federal agencies and their programs.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Requires the Commission to evaluate executive agencies and their programs.
Document 2: Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to create a separate website about the reports issued by each Office of Inspector General.
Document 3: Directs the President to designate two Commission co-chairpersons.
Requires the Commission to establish a systematic method for assessing the effectiveness and accountability of agency programs.

topic.name: Terrorism Detention Legislation
topic.description: Legislation concerning the detention and trial of individuals associated with terrorism in the United States and its territories.

Document 1: Due Process and Military Detention Amendments Act of 2013 - Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012
Prohibits any person detained, captured, or arrested in the United States... from being transferred to the custody of the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Detaining Terrorists to Protect Americans Act of 2016
This bill prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States... of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other detainee.

topic.name: Rail Storage Regulations
topic.description: Regulations governing the storage of rail cars by carriers, including requirements for storage plans and approvals.

Document 1: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan
approved by the Surface Transportation Board
Document 2: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan
approved by the Surface Transportation Board
Document 3: Transit System Flexibility Protection Act of 2007
public transportation formula grants for operating costs

topic.name: Bay Area Water Recycling Programs
topic.description: Legislative acts focused on the design, planning, and construction of recycled water systems and facilities in California's Bay Area.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution systems in California.
Limits the federal share of each project to 25%.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary to include in such agreements a provision for the reimbursement of construction costs, subject to appropriations made available for the federal share of the projects.
Increases the authorization of appropriations for the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities in cooperation with the city of Antioch, California.
Document 3: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water system facilities in California.
Prohibits the Secretary from providing funds for project operation and maintenance.

topic.name: Mercury Emissions Regulation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at controlling and reducing mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units under the Clean Air Act.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to propose regulations to control the emission of hazardous air pollutants, including mercury pollutants.
require a reduction of not less than 90% in such mercury emissions.
Document 3: Declares that this Act's purpose is to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate a rule that requires mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units to be subject to Maximum Achievable Control Technology (MACT) standards.
Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator to promulgate standards for mercury emissions from electric utility steam generating units.

topic.name: Employment Discrimination and Wage Equality
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at preventing discrimination in employment based on sexual orientation, gender identity, and sex, while promoting wage equality.

Document 1: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity
Prohibits related retaliation.
Document 2: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.

topic.name: Family Immigration Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss the Reuniting Families Act, which amends immigration laws to increase visa allocations for family-sponsored and employment-based immigrants.

Document 1: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000
Establishes the fiscal year worldwide level of family-sponsored immigrants at 480,000
Document 2: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000
Revises the definition of 'immediate relative' to mean a child, spouse, or parent of a U.S. citizen or lawful permanent resident
Document 3: Reuniting Families Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to establish the fiscal year worldwide level of employment-based immigrants at 140,000
Increases immigration visas for unmarried sons and daughters of U.S. citizens

topic.name: Military Survivor Benefits Reform
topic.description: Legislative changes addressing the offset of benefits for military surviving spouses and dependent children.

Document 1: Repeals certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities
Directs the Secretary concerned to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse who earlier agreed to transfer such eligibility to a surviving child or children of a member.
Document 2: Repeals, as of the later of October 1, 2007, or the date of enactment of this Act, certain provisions which require the offset of amounts paid in dependency and indemnity compensation from Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) annuities
Directs the Secretary to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse who earlier agreed to transfer such eligibility to a surviving child or children of a member.
Document 3: Repeals provisions phasing in the full concurrent receipt of such pay through December 31, 2013
Directs the Secretary concerned to restore annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse who earlier agreed to transfer such eligibility to a surviving child or children of a member.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension on Acrylic Fibers
topic.description: The documents discuss the extension of temporary duty suspensions on specific types of acrylic staple fibers in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units and 2 percent or more but not more than 3 percent of water
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing 85 percent or more by weight of acrylonitrile units and 2 percent or more but not over 3 percent of water
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fibers
containing at least 85 percent by weight of acrylonitrile units and 2 percent or more but not more than 3 percent of water

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislation that authorizes appropriations for U.S. intelligence and intelligence-related activities across various government agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence; (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2011, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: Requires funds appropriated for an intelligence or intelligence-related activity that are in excess of the amount specified for such activity in the funding tables accompanying the Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2012 to be specifically authorized by Congress.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence; (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2007, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.

topic.name: Television Broadcast Signal Retransmission
topic.description: Legislation related to the requirements for cable operators and satellite carriers to retransmit local and adjacent-market television broadcast signals in specified Wisconsin counties.

Document 1: require each satellite carrier providing secondary transmissions in the local market of a television broadcast station to carry, on request, the signals of all television broadcast stations located within any local market
subject to existing broadcast signal retransmission consent provisions.
Document 2: require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers in specified Wisconsin counties who elect to receive a television network
subjects satellite carriers to such requirements only to the extent that such retransmissions are technically feasible.
Document 3: require a cable operator or satellite carrier to provide to certain subscribers in specified Wisconsin counties who elect to receive a television network
exempts in-state, adjacent-market network station retransmissions in such counties from retransmission consent requirements

topic.name: Campaign Finance Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing transparency and disclosure requirements for campaign financing and political contributions.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
this requirement shall now apply to any political committee, not just a candidate's principal campaign committee
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to redefine the term 'independent expenditure'
prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations, labor organizations, and certain other entities
Document 3: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to redefine the term 'independent expenditure'
requires a covered organization which submits regular, periodic reports to its shareholders, members, or donors to include in each report

topic.name: Middle School Improvement Initiatives
topic.description: Legislative efforts aimed at enhancing educational outcomes for middle school students, particularly those from low-income backgrounds.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a majority of middle grade students matriculate to high schools with graduation rates below 60%
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Gives priority to LEAs, or partnerships that include LEAs, that serve high proportions of poor children and children attending eligible schools
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states, based on their proportion of poor children aged 5 to 17
Defines 'eligible schools' as those where a high proportion of middle grade students matriculate to secondary schools with graduation rates below 65%

topic.name: California Wilderness Designations
topic.description: The documents focus on the designation of various lands in California as wilderness areas and components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.

Document 1: Designates the following areas in California as wilderness areas
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate segments of the following as components of the national wild and scenic rivers system
Document 2: Designates as wilderness: certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands in the Cleveland National Forest
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate as wild, scenic, or recreational rivers specified
Document 3: Designates the following lands in California as wilderness: certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) lands in the Cleveland National Forest
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to manage such land as wilderness until it is incorporated into the Joshua Tree Wilderness

topic.name: Glass-Steagall Act Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to the Glass-Steagall Act aimed at separating commercial banking from investment banking and insurance activities.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Prohibits a national banking association from investing in structured or synthetic products.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.

Prohibits any individual who is an officer, director, partner, or employee of any securities entity, insurance company, or swaps entity from serving simultaneously as an officer of any insured depository institution.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Limits the purchase and sale of investment securities and stock by a national banking association to the accounts of customers.

topic.name: Federal Air Marshal Augmentation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing the number of federal air marshals to enhance transportation security for international flights.

Document 1: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750 above the number of such marshals as of January 31, 2010.
Declares the goal of this Act is to increase the number of inbound flights with federal marshals onboard.
Document 2: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750 above the number of such marshals as of January 31, 2011.
Requires federal air marshals hired before enactment of this Act who have not completed such program to attend an alternative training program.
Document 3: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) for the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to increase the number of federal air marshals by at least an additional 1,750 above the number of such marshals as of January 31, 2010.

Directs the Federal Air Marshal Service (FAMS) to establish a policy requiring newly hired federal air marshals to complete as part of their basic training the criminal investigative training program.

topic.name: Regulation of Health Hazards
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating substances that pose health risks to humans, particularly in food and drug contexts.

Document 1: Preservation of Antibiotics for Medical Treatment Act of 2009 - Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health due to the development of antimicrobial resistance.
Defines 'critical antimicrobial animal drug' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals that contains specified antibiotics or other drugs used in humans.
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide that, in determining whether a controlled substance analogue was intended for human consumption, the following factors may be considered: the marketing, advertising, and labeling of the substance.

Declares that evidence that a substance was not marketed, advertised, or labeled for human consumption shall not by itself be sufficient to establish that the substance was not intended for human consumption.
Document 3: Prohibits the distribution of a food if its container is composed, in whole or in part, of bisphenol A (BPA) or can release BPA into food.

Requires the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to review substances in order to determine whether there is a reasonable certainty that no harm will result from aggregate exposure to such substance.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing labor practices, manufacturing standards, and recognition of historical figures in government.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to eliminate the consumptive demand exception
prohibition on the importation of goods made with convict labor, forced labor, or indentured labor.
Document 2: Requires any flags of the United States acquired for use by the federal government
entirely manufactured in the United States from articles, materials, or supplies entirely grown, produced, or manufactured in the United States.
Document 3: Directs the Postal Service to provide for the issuance and sale of a semipostal
to afford the public a convenient means to contribute towards the acquisition of works of art honoring female pioneers in government service.

topic.name: Small Business Government Contracting
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing opportunities and support for small businesses in federal contracting processes.

Document 1: increases the government-wide goal for awards to small businesses from 23% to 24%
goal for award to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
Document 2: authorize a contracting opportunity to be awarded under the HUBZone program
establish mentor-protege programs for small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans and women
Document 3: establish a training certification program for federal contracting officers
contracting under the Small Business Innovation Research Program

topic.name: Housing Finance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and facilitating housing finance, particularly through refinancing and tenant protections during foreclosures.

Document 1: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Defines a qualified mortgage as one that is an existing first mortgage for purchase of, or refinancing another first mortgage on, a one- to four-family dwelling.
Document 2: States that any immediate successor in interest to residential property in foreclosure assumes such interest subject to giving the existing bona fide tenant at least 90-day notice to vacate.

Amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to require a housing assistance payment contract to provide that in the case of an owner who is an immediate successor in interest pursuant to foreclosure, certain tenant rights are preserved.
Document 3: Directs the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) to carry out a one-year program providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.
Specifies the terms and conditions of a refinancing mortgage, including a 50-year term to maturity and a prohibition on borrower fees.

topic.name: Land Exchange Legislation
topic.description: Legislation governing the exchange of federal and non-federal land involving the Sugar Loaf Fire Protection District in Colorado.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to accept the offer and to convey federal land in the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests in Colorado.
Expresses the intent of Congress that such land exchange be completed within one year of enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer and convey federal land in the Arapaho-Roosevelt National Forests.
Sets forth requirements for the disposition and use of the proceeds received as a result of any cash equalization payment or any sale specified above.
Document 3: Makes provisions relating to the exchange of public lands and interests within the National Forest System under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 applicable to the land exchange authorized by this Act.
Allows the Secretary to offer to sell the federal land to the District if the exchange is not completed by that deadline.
